titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Scott Alexander, 4 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Andrew Phillip Magill (1871-1941), private secretary to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary for Ireland. He is responding to Scott Alexander on behalf of Birrell to acknowledge the receipt of his letter asking for a job.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/4172

correspAction.placeName: 24 Buckingham Street, Strand, W.C., London, United Kingdom

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
4th March, 1916. Dear Sir, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge
the receipt of your letter of the 3rd instant,
and to express his regret that he is not aware
of any vacancy at present existing in connection
with which your claims could be considered.
A note has, however, been taken of your application
in case a suitable vacancy does arise. Yours faithfully, (Sd.) APM.
Under Secy.
A.P.M.
4/3 Scott Alexander, Esq., 24, Buckingham Street, Strand, W.C. Under Secy
A.P.M
4/3 I
MN
5/3 Report in T.D.
E.O.F.
7-III-16
